content: The 'spelling/lexical reflexion' entry from the high school English vocabulary curriculum can be used as a lightweight bootstrapping benchmark for LLM self-reflection mechanisms: an LLM is first asked to generate its own definition and usage of 'reflexion', then compare its output against the standardized entry to self-correct any misspellings, semantic gaps, or contextual biases. This reveals that LLM self-reflection can be improved by grounding abstract self-correction routines in explicit, human-curated lexical reflection conventions.
